title: Analysis of Early Promoters of theBacillus Bacteriophage GA-1
summary: The paper characterizes bacteriophage GA-1, which infects Bacillus sp. strain G1R, and compares it to bacteriophage φ29, which infects Bacillus subtilis. GA-1 has unique promoters that are active early in infection and repressed later by protein p6, similar to φ29. Both phages share evolutionary relationships and functional similarities in their protein p6-mediated transcriptional repression mechanisms.

Bacteriophage GA-1.growth_medium: Luria-Bertani medium supplemented with 5 mM MgSO4

extra.protein_p6_function: represses early promoters A1c, A1a, C1b, and C2
extra.transcription_assay_conditions: 25 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5), 10 mM MgCl2, 2 mM dithiothreitol, 200 μM each nucleoside triphosphate, 7.5 U of RNasin, 0.2 nM genomic GA-1 DNA, 200 nM ςA RNA polymerase

extra.protein_p6_function: binds DNA with low sequence specificity, prefers regions with anisotropic bendability, can bind entire genome under certain conditions
extra.protein_p6_purification: purified from B. subtilis 110NA as described previously
